title: Some Containers Are Not Accessible by the Outside Network
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

Some containers cannot be accessed from outside the network. Others can.  The ones that can't be accessed are fully accessible from inside the network.  

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create container

2. Open Firewall and create virtual machine config in modem

3. Try to access it from another network

**Describe the results you received:**

Containers function fully inside the network but not outside

**Describe the results you expected:**

Containers to function seamlessly between inside and outside networks

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
linuxserver/heimdall (port 4040) works from outside the network
miniflux/miniflux (port 7878) bitwardenrs/server (port 8989) and linuxserver/swag (port 443) cannot be accessed from the outside

My arm64 machine doesn't have this issue
**Output of `podman version`:**

podman version 3.3.1
**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Fedora IoT 34 arm32. Fresh new system. Same configs worked before on the same OS before reinstalling. 
Firewalld is well configured and all the relevant ports are open.

comments.body: Is this Podman on a MAC?
MacOS you mean? No, it's on Fedora IoT
@mheon @Luap99 PTAL
Can you share the podman commands you use? Do you run the containers rootless or rootful?

Container that doesn't work:

`podman run -d  --security-opt label=disable --label "io.containers.autoupdate=image" --name=swag --cap-add=NET_ADMIN -e PUID=1000 -e PGID=1000 -e TZ=Asia/Beirut -e URL=myfancywebsite.net -e SUBDOMAINS=  -v ~/.swag:/config  -e EMAIL=antoine.saroufim@someprovider.com  -p 443:443  linuxserver/swag:latest`


Container that works:

`podman run -d --security-opt label=disable  --label "io.containers.autoupdate=image" --name=heimdall  -e PUID=1000  -e PGID=1000  -e TZ=Asia/Beirut  -p 4040:443  -v ~/.heimdall:/config    ghcr.io/linuxserver/heimdall:latest`
What is the container with NET_ADMIN attempting to do,  if you are running in rootless mode, there is not much network administration that would be allowed.
Tend to concur - it looks like it might be attempting to alter the host's network config, which isn't going to work with a rootless container
That capability is for fail2ban to function. Even if it is removed, the webserver (nginx) should still be able to function
The other major difference is `-p 443:443` vs `-p 4040:443` - if you change the port on the first container, does anything change?
I actually tried switching these two containers. Heimdall worked fine on port 443 and swag didn't on port 4040. I even redirected port 443 from the modem to port 4443. That didn't help
It's also noteworthy that Bitwarden's container doesn't use any capabilities and uses port 8989 yet it would still not connect from the outside
Is traffic to those ports allowed on the host's firewall?

Yes, the ports were opened via

`firewall-cmd --zone=public --add-port={4040,443}/tcp
`
then

`firewall-cmd --zone=public --permanent --add-port={4040,443} `
Can you access the services via localhost on the system running podman?
Can you give me an example of the command that I can use to accomplish this?
If it's a web service, just doing a `curl` against localhost on the appropriate port could work?

Container 1 ``` curl localhost:7878 404 page not found ``` Container 2 (bitwarden) `Bitwarden Web Vault

`

It looks like it is accessible locally, which is interesting...

Can you stop the containers, turn off firewalld, restart them, and see if it works?

Just did that and I had the same effect. However, I noticed after this that all containers can be reached if I use my domain name + port instead of /subfolder. Now all of them can't be reached through the /subfolder including the ones on other machines. This seems like an issue with the reverse proxy container and not all containers on one machine.
Closing this as it turns out to be a modem issue.
